messages.content: Explain quantum computing in simple terms

Quantum computing uses qubits instead of classical bits. While a regular bit is either 0 or 1, a qubit can be both at the same time (superposition). This allows quantum computers to process many possibilities simultaneously.
Entanglement is when two qubits become linked — measuring one instantly determines the other, no matter the distance. Einstein called it "spooky action at a distance".
